title: brain graduation migrate [--apply | --rollback] [--export-challenge | --authorization <path>] — --json success output
description: Privileged, deterministic, review-gated bootstrap migration of the graduation copy per bootstrap-migration.md: assign stable ids, infer type by the contract precedence, initialize schema_version 1, preserve/rewrite links, quarantine identity conflicts, refuse unsupported schema_version + unknown/malformed explicit type. Per-note checkpoints (with BOTH pre AND post images persisted before the atomic rename) make it idempotent + resumable; --rollback reverts via those checkpoints. Non-mutating preview by default. BOTH mutating modes (--apply and --rollback) are Tier-3 review-gated and authorized by the same broker op (graduation migrate, security-broker-contract.md §7.4/§7.5) via THREE distinct flows: (1) an interactive OS-presence assertion bound to the challenge in-process, (2) the --export-challenge → sign → --authorization out-of-band flow, or (3) a supplied --authorization; --yes never authorizes. --apply is a forward graduate binding intendedEffect{fromGeneration,toGeneration,migrationPlanDigest}; --rollback is the SAME op in the REVERSE direction — a graduate whose fromGeneration is the current (migrated) generation, toGeneration is the pre-migration generation, and migrationPlanDigest is the reversal plan digest (the broker SSOT's GraduateEffect, no rollback-specific fields). A stale rollback whose current generation no longer equals the challenge fromGeneration is rejected authz.generation_mismatch; a drifted reversal plan authz.migration_plan_mismatch; a replayed authorization authz.nonce_replayed. Exit 6 only when presence is unavailable/declined (and no --authorization) or a challenge is exported with --export-challenge (which emits an AuthorizationChallenge instead of this object). Runs under the vault-maintenance lock, agent-branch-only, never on main-vault.

mode.description: preview ⇒ no mutation, no audit-ref event; applied ⇒ broker-authorized mutation; rolled-back ⇒ per-note checkpoint reversal.

migrationRunId.description: ULID identifying this bootstrap run (checkpoint key).

initializedFrontmatter.description: The managed frontmatter migration deterministically writes so the migrated note satisfies the canonical reader's required set (apps/cli vault frontmatter schema): id, type, schema_version, title, created, updated. created/updated come from the copy's git history for the path, deterministically falling back to the config bootstrapTimestamp when the path has no history (§1: a pure function of input vault + config — no wall-clock).

released.description: Present when an incompatible-link note migrated because an operator authorized its release as-is (link preserved verbatim as prose).

quarantined.description: Notes routed to quarantine (identity/alias/link conflicts); resolved with quarantine inspect|resolve.

refused.description: Notes refused (never mutated): an unsupported schema_version, or an explicit `type` outside the managed set (unknown-type — bootstrap-migration.md §3, since migration owns the `type` key it can neither overwrite the asserted value nor guess).

assertedType.description: The unmanaged explicit type value that triggered an unknown-type refusal (serialized as a string).

idMap.description: { repo-relative path → assigned id } for every migrated note.

rolledBack.description: Present in mode=rolled-back: per-note reversal outcomes (reverse sorted-path order), each restoring the checkpoint pre-image ONLY after the note's current on-disk bytes are verified to still hash to the recorded postImageSha256 (a conflicting post-migration edit is refused, not clobbered — see rollbackConflicts).

restoredToSha256.description: sha256 of the restored pre-image bytes (equals the checkpoint preImageSha256 — a byte-exact reversal).

rollbackConflicts.description: Present in mode=rolled-back: notes whose CURRENT on-disk bytes no longer hash to the recorded postImageSha256 (a post-migration edit landed). Rollback FAILS CLOSED for these — the pre-image is NOT restored and the newer bytes are left untouched for operator review (bootstrap-migration.md §8.2).

rollbackOrder.description: Present in mode=rolled-back: the reverse sorted-path order in which notes were processed for reversal.

releases.description: Authorized incompatible-link releases: notes that migrated with an unresolved link left verbatim because an operator authorized the release (quarantine resolve --resolution release).

authorization.description: Identifier of the authorization that released the item.

x-atlas-contract.summary: Broker-authorized, plan-digest-bound bootstrap migration; deterministic ids/types/links, per-note checkpoints (idempotent/resumable), rollback; preview by default, Tier-3 review-gated apply.

flags.description: Mutate the copy (Tier-3, broker-authorized); without it the command is a non-mutating preview emitting no audit-ref event.

Revert a prior bootstrap via the per-note checkpoints (restoring each note's verified pre-image), in reverse sorted-path order (idempotent). A rollback whose current on-disk bytes no longer match a note's recorded postImageSha256 FAILS CLOSED for that note (a post-migration edit is never clobbered). MUTATING + broker-authorized like --apply — the SAME op (graduation migrate) in the reverse direction, binding fromGeneration (current migrated generation) / toGeneration (pre-migration generation) / migrationPlanDigest (reversal plan). A stale rollback whose current generation no longer equals the challenge fromGeneration is rejected authz.generation_mismatch; a replayed authorization authz.nonce_replayed. Without an interactive presence assertion or --authorization it exits 6.

Emit the broker AuthorizationChallenge to stdout and exit 6 instead of mutating; for --apply bound to intendedEffect{fromGeneration,toGeneration,migrationPlanDigest}, for --rollback the same fields in the reverse direction (reversal plan digest). Sign it out of band. Omitting it uses the interactive OS-presence flow instead.

Path to a signed AuthorizationResponse from a prior --export-challenge; presented to the broker to authorize --apply OR --rollback. Optional when the interactive OS-presence flow satisfies the challenge in-process.
Caller-supplied idempotency key; a repeat --apply with the same key + request-hash (requestHashScope) returns the existing result rather than re-migrating (registry idempotency: key-accepting).

sideEffects: on --apply: writes managed frontmatter (id/type/schema_version/title/created/updated) + rewritten links to the copy
persists per-note checkpoints with verified pre/post images + rollback progress (resumable, byte-exact reversible)
on --rollback: restores each migrated note's pre-image (reverse sorted-path order) and marks it pending
finalizeLedgerWrite (run.integrated on --apply; a distinct rollback run on --rollback)
prohibitedEffects: never mutates the live main-vault (copy + agent-branch only)
never authorized by --yes; never bypasses the broker challenge
never silently suffixes a duplicate explicit id (quarantines it)
never downgrades or rewrites an unsupported schema_version (refuses it)
preview never mutates and never emits an audit-ref event

errorCodes.when: unknown flag/argument, --dry-run with --apply, --apply combined with --rollback, or --export-challenge combined with --authorization
--apply OR --rollback when the interactive OS-presence authorization is unavailable or declined and no --authorization is supplied, or a challenge was exported with --export-challenge

broker drift: current generation != challenge fromGeneration — for --apply the forward from-generation, for --rollback the current (migrated) generation the reverse graduate starts from; a stale rollback against a moved generation (security-broker-contract.md §7.3)
broker drift (--apply): re-derived migration plan digest differs from the challenge
broker: the challenge nonce expired (expiresAt passed)
broker: signingPayload differs from the broker-recomputed canonical bytes
broker: the challenge/response fails the contracts schema (universal broker validation failure)
broker: the response's payloadCanonicalization id is unknown (universal broker validation failure)
broker: signerId is not in the signer registry
broker: the signer is not permitted for this op
broker: the nonce was not issued by this broker
broker: the nonce was already consumed
the graduation scan gate is blocked or absent
the fail-closed backup watermark blocks a ledger-writing run
